---
title: Scale your AI context for coding agents
description: Scale your AI context for coding agents. Make your next context work (skills, plugins & more) for your team, community, or self. Context engineering toolkit for AI coding agents.
template: splash
hero:
  title: Scale your AI context for coding agents
  tagline: Make your next context work (skills, plugins & more) for your team, community, or self. Context engineering toolkit for AI coding agents.
  actions:
    - text: Get Started
      link: /get-started/
      icon: right-arrow
    - text: View on GitHub
      link: https://github.com/saif-shines/doraval
      variant: minimal
      icon: external
    - text: JSR
      link: https://jsr.io/@hacksmith/doraval
      variant: minimal
      icon: external
  image:
    html: |
      <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Doraemon mascot" class="doraemon-hero" />
---

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<div class="home-fold">

<div class="home-fold-install">

<span class="home-fold-label">Install</span>

<Tabs>
  <TabItem label="macOS (Homebrew)">
    ```bash
    brew tap saif-shines/tap
    brew trust saif-shines/tap
    brew install doraval
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="npm">
    Global install:

    ```bash
    npm install -g @hacksmith/doraval
    ```

    Or run without installing:

    ```bash
    npx @hacksmith/doraval validate .
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="Bun">
    Global install:

    ```bash
    bun add -g @hacksmith/doraval
    ```

    Or run without installing:

    ```bash
    bunx @hacksmith/doraval validate .
    ```
  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

<p class="home-fold-hint">After install: <code>dora validate .</code> on your project.</p>

</div>

<div class="home-fold-demo">

<span class="home-fold-label">Try it — no install</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">Validate a popular plugin on GitHub — clone, auto-detect validators, and see what passes.</p>

```bash
npx @hacksmith/doraval validate https://github.com/obra/superpowers
```

```text
  Cloning https://github.com/obra/superpowers...

  dora validate — 8 validator(s)
  Path:  https://github.com/obra/superpowers
  8 validators • 0 errors • 3 warnings

  Claude Plugin (claude:plugin)
  Status  Check
  ✓  name: "superpowers"
  ✓  version: "6.0.3"
  ✓  keywords: [skills, tdd, debugging, collaboration, ...]
  ✓  skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md exists
  ✓  skills/test-driven-development/SKILL.md exists
  … 12 more skills checked
  Result: 0 error(s), 1 warning(s)
```

<p class="home-fold-links">Repo: <a href="https://github.com/obra/superpowers">obra/superpowers</a> · <a href="/commands/validate/">validate command</a></p>

</div>

</div>

## The toolkit

Same workflow as the [quickstart](/get-started/quickstart/): scaffold, validate, journal, **eval**. Validate is above — each card shows the command and what you get back. Use `npx` before install; use `dora` after.

<div class="home-commands">

<div class="home-command">

<span class="home-fold-label">Scaffold</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">Context-aware wizard — detects your directory, picks standalone vs plugin, writes the right layout for Claude Code.</p>

```bash
npx @hacksmith/doraval claude new --yes --intent self my-skill
```

```text
  doraval claude new — Context-aware scaffolding
  Decision: path=standalone, target=./my-skill

  ✓ Created standalone at ./my-skill
  Command: /my-skill
  Validate: dora validate ./my-skill
```

<p class="home-fold-links">Also: <code>cursor new</code>, <code>codex new</code>, <code>copilot new</code> · <a href="/commands/claude-new/">scaffold docs</a></p>

</div>

<div class="home-command">

<span class="home-fold-label">Drift</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">Session-grounded behavioral analysis — did the agent actually follow the skill in real transcripts? Rank skills by drift rate.</p>

```bash
npx @hacksmith/doraval drift ./skills/my-skill/
# or: dora drift   (repo sweep, highest drift first)
```

```text
  dora drift — session-grounded behavioral analysis

  Skill: brainstorming   Sessions checked: 5   Sessions matched: 3

  Session a1b2c3 "Design a new feature"
    ↗ DRIFTED     Run git log for churn signal   (no git log call found)
    ? UNCLEAR     Ask clarifying questions first  (instruction too vague)

  Aggregate drift rate: 1/8 binding instructions drifted (12%)
```

<p class="home-fold-links"><a href="/commands/drift/">drift command</a> · <a href="/concepts/three-tier-verification/">three-tier verification</a> · needs sessions + <a href="/commands/init/">init</a></p>

</div>

<div class="home-command">

<span class="home-fold-label">Providers</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">See manifest paths and keyword discovery per agent before you ship cross-platform context.</p>

```bash
npx @hacksmith/doraval providers
```

```text
  doraval providers — Supported platforms

  claude — Claude Code
  Manifest: .claude-plugin/plugin.json
  Keywords in plugin.json: supported — triggers in Claude Code
  Example: dora validate . --for claude:plugin

  cursor — Cursor
  Manifest: .cursor-plugin/plugin.json
  Keywords in plugin.json: supported — triggers in Cursor
  Example: dora validate . --for cursor:plugin

  … codex, copilot, grok
```

<p class="home-fold-links"><a href="/commands/providers/">providers command</a></p>

</div>

<div class="home-command">

<span class="home-fold-label">Journal</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">The journal remembers decisions and principles while scaling AI context (skills, plugins, etc.). Run <a href="/commands/init/">dora init</a> once to set up decision memory + agent integration.</p>

```bash
dora journal add "Validate before shipping any skill change"
dora journal list
```

```text
  dora journal list — my-project

   1  Validate before shipping any skill change
      tags: workflow, quality
      by: human  on 2026-06-20
      Catch breakage before scaling context to the team.

   2  Use drift not score for rubric deviation
      tags: naming, cli
      by: human  on 2026-05-25
```

<p class="home-fold-links"><a href="/commands/journal-list/">journal list</a> · <a href="/concepts/agent-journal/">Agent Journal</a></p>

</div>

<div class="home-command home-command-wide">

<span class="home-fold-label">Judge</span>

<p class="home-fold-lead">Artifact vs rubric in one LLM call — no sessions. <code>eval</code> / <code>evals</code> are aliases. Use <a href="/commands/drift/">drift</a> for session behavioral analysis.</p>

```bash
dora judge ./skills/my-skill/
# aliases: dora eval ./skills/my-skill/   dora evals ./skills/my-skill/
```

```text
  [PASS] Rubric alignment
  All mandatory criteria met: activation phrase, ordered steps, imperative voice,
  code examples, and explicit MUST / MUST NOT guardrails.
```

<p class="home-fold-links"><a href="/commands/judge/">judge command</a> · <a href="/concepts/three-tier-verification/">three-tier verification</a> · needs <a href="/commands/init/">init</a> for model config</p>

</div>

</div>

<p class="home-toolkit-more">Also: <a href="/commands/init/">init</a> (one-time journal + agent setup) · <a href="/commands/ui/">ui</a> (local dashboard). Full path in the <a href="/get-started/quickstart/">quickstart</a>.</p>
